paper_title: Soil Chronosequence Study in Kirgizskiy Range

methods.description: This method involves the examination of soil profiles to understand the changes in soil types from the foothills to the top slope of the Tatyr, Chon-Kurchak, Kurchak-Tor valleys. It aims to reconstruct past climatic conditions and soil formation processes over the last 10,000-13,000 years.

steps.description: Study 40 soil profiles representing the change of soil types from the foothills to the top slope.
steps.searchable_summary: collect data from 40 soil profiles across different elevations

steps.description: Analyze the morphology and organic matter properties of the Chernozem-like soils formed on the moraines of the Shopocov glacier.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze soil morphology and organic matter properties
outputs: morphological and organic matter analysis results

steps.description: Determine the radiocarbon age of soil horizons to understand the timeline of soil formation.
steps.searchable_summary: perform radiocarbon dating on soil horizons

steps.description: Reconstruct past climatic conditions using δ13C values and humic acid composition.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze δ13C values and humic acid composition for climate reconstruction
